label: Options Trade Panel
description: Order entry for options, spreads, and combos selected from the chain or strategy builder.

0.1.note: WidgetErrorBoundary in widget-wrapper.tsx covers all grid widgets.

0.4.note: No any types or @ts-ignore in widget file.

0.5.note: Widget file has no lib/mocks/ imports; TradePanel sub-component imports placeMockOrder and options-futures-mock constants internally.

0.6.note: options-data-context has no isLoading field; data is synchronous mock.

0.7.note: TradePanel renders selection prompt when instrument===null; all form controls disabled when noInstrument===true.

0.8.note: Context throws if Provider missing (WidgetErrorBoundary); TradePanel has no async error path.

0.9.note: No dead code or commented-out blocks in widget file.

l0.notes: 0.6/0.8 N/A — synchronous mock data, no async paths.

1.1.note: Widget reads selectedInstrument exclusively via useOptionsData(). TradePanel calls placeMockOrder directly — dual submit path (context also has handleSubmitOrder). Tracked in knownIssues.

1.3.note: Org filter scopes watchlists; instrument selection is user-driven.

1.4.note: clientIds not applicable — order entry is instrument-scoped.

1.5.note: Strategy filter not applicable to individual instrument order entry.

1.6.note: Empty organizationIds returns full watchlist scope.

1.7.note: placeMockOrder from lib/api/mock-trade-ledger used inside TradePanel — should route through context in a future refactor.

l1.notes: TradePanel dual-submit path (bypasses context handleSubmitOrder) — tracked in knownIssues.

l2.notes: Covered globally by commits 32ba8ab + f9f29ea. Re-audit only if entitlements change.

3.1.note: Mode relevance: live ✓ (live order entry), paper ✓ (paper order submission), batch — order entry should be disabled in batch mode.

3.2.note: Context reads mode from useExecutionMode() at context line 129; mode available in context value.

3.5.note: Paper mode: submit routes to paper ledger via placeMockOrder (mock currently handles all modes identically — production will branch on mode).

l3.notes: 3.3/3.4 require human verification (submit disabled in batch, batch indicator visible).

4.1.note: No dead onClick handlers in widget file; TradePanel sub-component has functional submit handler.

l4.notes: 4.3/4.4/4.7/4.8 require human walkthrough (direction, order type, qty, price, submit).

5.1.note: Target strategies: options-buying (long call/put), options-selling (short call/put), vertical-spread, iron-condor, options-combo. Widget serves order entry for all instrument types (option, combo, spread). Receives selection from options-chain or options-strategies.

7.1.note: No hardcoded hex/rgb/hsl colors in widget file.

7.2.note: No mock data imports in widget file.

7.3.note: Widget file is 10 lines; well within limits.

7.5.note: Out of scope — English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21; 7.5 reassigned to UTC datetime rendering

7.6.note: No security anti-patterns in widget file.

7.7.note: No hooks or deps in widget file; pure prop pass-through render.

8.1.note: No text-[...] or fontSize= in widget file.

8.2.note: No arbitrary spacing tokens in widget file.

8.3.note: Widget delegates entirely to TradePanel from shared trading panel.

8.4.note: Agent grep: no bare div onClick, no img without alt in widget file. Human keyboard pass for TradePanel form required.

keepSeparate.rationale: Trade panel and options chain have very different sizes and layout roles; merging would harm grid flexibility.

relationships.notes: Receives selectedInstrument from OptionsDataContext (driven by options-chain or options-strategies widget selection).

dataContract.notes: Form widget — no continuous data stream. Mark price populated from selectedInstrument (driven by options-chain WebSocket in production). Order submission is REST on-demand.

gaps: Combo leg display limited — shows single-instrument style for combo types
coverage.notes: Handles single options, futures, spread instruments, and combo instruments via TradePanel type switch.

knownIssues: TradePanel calls placeMockOrder directly (4 separate call sites for option/future/spread/combo modes) rather than delegating to context handleSubmitOrder — dual submit path.

TradePanel manages its own local form state (direction, orderType, price, amount) rather than using context state (tradeDirection, orderType, orderQty, orderPrice already in context). Creates state duplication.

findings.description: options-trade-panel-widget.tsx — TradePanel bypasses context handleSubmitOrder and calls placeMockOrder directly with its own local form state. Context already exposes tradeDirection, orderType, orderQty, orderPrice, handleSubmitOrder.
findings.deferReason: TradePanel refactor (consolidate form state into context) deferred to BP-5 data wiring phase.

findings.description: options-trade-panel-widget.tsx — 0 direct user-visible strings in widget file; strings in TradePanel sub-component
findings.fix: Retired 7.5 i18n check: English-only UI per product decision 2026-04-21
